meta.title: Free QR Code Generator Online | PNG, SVG, JPEG, WebP | TeamCellMania
meta.description: Create free custom QR codes: URL, text and WiFi. Download as PNG, SVG, JPEG or WebP up to 2048×2048px. Add your logo, choose colors and styles. No sign-up.
meta.title_wifi: Free WiFi QR Code Generator | Download PNG SVG | TeamCellMania
meta.title_url: Free URL QR Code Generator | PNG SVG WebP up to 2048px | TeamCellMania
meta.title_text: Free Text QR Code Generator | PNG SVG JPEG WebP | TeamCellMania
meta.og_image_alt: Free QR code generator — download as PNG, SVG, JPEG or WebP with logo and colors — TeamCellMania

header.subtitle: Customize colors, shapes and logo in seconds

url.label: URL
url.hint: Link to a website, social media or any web address

text.label: Text
text.hint: Message, phone number or any free text

wifi.label: WiFi
wifi.hint: Share your network so others can connect by scanning

form.content_tooltip_url: Enter the full web address including https://. For example: https://your-business.com or https://wa.me/1234567890
form.content_tooltip_text: Any text you want to encode: a message, phone number, address, etc.
form.content_hint_url: Always include https:// so it opens the browser when scanned.
form.content_hint_text: Recommended max: 300 characters for best QR quality.
form.model_tooltip: Defines the visual shape of the QR modules. All styles are equally scannable; pick the one that fits your brand best.
form.dots_color_tooltip: The color of the QR modules. Use dark colors on a light background so scanners can detect it properly.
form.bg_color_tooltip: The background color of the QR. White or very light colors work best for reliable scanning.
form.qr_margin_tooltip: White space around the QR code. A minimum of 10px helps scanners detect it correctly, especially when printed.
form.img_margin_tooltip: Space between your center logo and the QR dots. If the logo covers too much of the code, increase this value.
form.image_applied_hint: It will appear in the center of the QR

wifi.ssid_tooltip: The name that appears in the list of available WiFi networks. Exactly as written, including uppercase letters.
wifi.ssid_hint: Copy the exact network name, including uppercase and spaces.
wifi.password_tooltip: Your WiFi password. When scanned, the device will connect automatically without typing it.
wifi.password_hint: Leave empty if your network has no password.
wifi.security_tooltip: Your network's security protocol. Most modern networks use WPA/WPA2. If unsure, try WPA first.
wifi.security_hint: WPA/WPA2 is most common on modern routers.
wifi.hidden_tooltip: Enable this only if your router is configured to not broadcast the network name. Most networks are NOT hidden.
wifi.hidden_hint: Usually off. Only use if your network doesn't appear in the WiFi list.

preview.title: Preview
preview.format_tooltip: PNG has background and is most compatible. SVG is vector (ideal for printing at any size). JPEG is lighter but no transparency.
preview.size_tooltip: Larger size = better print quality. 300×300 is fine for screens; use 1200×1200 for professional printing.

steps.title: Choose the QR type
steps.description: Select the content type: URL for web links, Text for messages, or WiFi to share your network easily.

steps.title: Fill in the content
steps.description: Fill in the fields based on the type chosen. For URL enter the web address; for WiFi, the network name and password.

steps.title: Customize the style
steps.description: Choose the dot shape by scrolling the options and adjust the colors. All styles are equally scannable.

steps.title: Add your logo (optional)
steps.description: Upload an image to appear in the center of the QR. Works great with brand logos. Max 5 MB.

steps.title: Download your QR
steps.description: Click 'Generate QR' to see the preview, then 'Download PNG' to save it in high quality, ready to use.

download.format_tooltip: PNG for general use, SVG for lossless scaling (ideal for printing), JPEG for lighter files, WebP for modern web.
download.size_tooltip: The pixel size of the downloaded image. Use 1024 or 2048 for printing; 512 is enough for web or social media.
